Meta.description: Centralized dependency-vulnerability monitoring across your portfolio.

Home.projectsAllClearDescription: No projects need attention right now. Toggle “Show healthy” above to browse the full list.
Home.projectsNoneConfiguredDescription: Add a root in Settings → Roots and let the worker discover projects on its next sweep.
Home.librariesEmptyDescription: Adjust filters above, or wait for the next scan to populate findings.

roots.emptyDescription: Add a filesystem path the worker should walk. Discovered projects appear in the catalog on the next sweep.
roots.scanTitle: Walk this root and scan its projects

dialog.title: Add root
dialog.description: Pick a folder the worker should walk. Discovered projects will appear in the catalog on the next sweep.
dialog.alreadyConfigured: This path is already configured as a root.

deleteConfirm.title: Remove root?
deleteConfirm.description: Permanently remove {path}? {projectCount, plural, =0 {No projects are linked to this root.} one {# project under it will also be deleted, along with its scans, findings, and notifications.} other {# projects under it will also be deleted, along with their scans, findings, and notifications.}} This cannot be undone.

schedule.cadenceHelp: The worker walks each configured root and runs npm audit on every discovered project at this cadence.
schedule.startHourHelp: For intervals longer than 1h, anchors the time of day scans start (worker local time).
schedule.timezoneHelp: The timezone the start hour is interpreted in. Defaults to the server's timezone.

notifications.emptyDescription: Add a Slack webhook, Telegram bot, or generic webhook.
notifications.testTitle: Live test — POSTs a short message to verify the target works
notifications.historyAria: Send historical events to this target
notifications.historyTitle: Queue every existing event for dispatch to this target on the next tick
notifications.duplicateTitle: Create a copy of this target — credentials carry over so you only tweak filters / scope
notifications.historyQueued: Queued {count, plural, one {# historical event} other {# historical events}} for backfill. The worker will dispatch on its next tick.

prod.label: Production only
prod.description: Fire only for findings on a production dependency.

all.label: Both
all.description: Fire for every finding regardless of where it was pulled in from.

dev.label: Development only
dev.description: Fire only for findings reachable solely from devDependencies.

dialog.title: Add notification target
dialog.description: Slack, Telegram, or a generic webhook. Severities you tick will be delivered to this target.

deleteConfirm.title: Remove notification target?
deleteConfirm.description: Permanently remove the notification target {identity}? Existing delivery history will be kept, but no further events will be sent to this target.

notifications.webhookFlavorHelp: JSON sends root, project and vulnerabilities for an auto-fix agent. Plain-text sends the advisory export, ready to feed an LLM.

defaults.depViewHelp: Controls what shows when you open the home dashboard or a project / library detail page without an explicit filter in the URL. Per-page filter selections always override this.

prod.label: Production only
prod.description: Hide findings reachable only from devDependencies. Recommended for ship-quality views.

all.label: All dependencies
all.description: Show every finding, regardless of where it was pulled in from.

dev.label: Dev only
dev.description: Only show findings reachable solely from devDependencies. Useful when triaging tooling.

defaults.minSeverityHelp: Applied as the starting value of the severity filter on home and detail views.
defaults.sortHelp: Applied as the starting sort order for project and library tables.
defaults.savedHint: Changes apply immediately to every page that reads defaults.

export.title: Advisory export prompt
export.description: This text is prepended to every Markdown advisory export (from a project or library detail page). It frames how the dev team — or an LLM acting on their behalf — should approach the fixes. Edit it to match your team's policy. If you clear it or reset to default, exports fall back to the built-in prompt.

advanced.portalBaseUrl: Portal base URL (used in notification deep links)
advanced.watcherLabel: Lockfile watcher (scan-on-change) — master switch. Worker enqueues a scan_request when a watched lockfile changes.
advanced.noRoots: No roots configured yet. Add a root under Settings → Roots first.
advanced.emptySelectionHelp: An empty selection means the watcher will not run — even with the master switch on.
advanced.dryRunLabel: Dry-run notifications — log what would dispatch, never POST to external targets.
advanced.notificationLanguageHelp: Language used for scan-failure notifications (Slack, Telegram, webhooks).

scope.label: Scope
scope.allRootsHelp: Fires for every root, including ones added in the future.
scope.selectedRootsHelp: Only fires for the roots and/or projects you pick below.
scope.noRoots: No roots configured yet — add one in Settings → Roots.
scope.selectionError: Pick at least one root or project, or switch to <all>All</all>.

Findings.projectsAffected: {count, plural, one {# project affected} other {# projects affected}}

alias.title: Edit display name
alias.description: Override the folder name in the UI. Leave empty to fall back to the folder name.
alias.label: Display name

tags.title: Edit tags
tags.description: Comma-separated. Tags help group projects in the catalog.
tags.label: Tags

mute.findingDescription: Silences this specific advisory for this project. Other findings keep notifying.
mute.projectDescription: Silences ALL findings and scan failures for this project until you unmute or the mute expires.
mute.reasonPlaceholderFinding: e.g. waiting on upstream fix in package@2.x

muteLibrary.allMutedTrigger: All advisories on this library are already muted
muteLibrary.muteAllTrigger: Mute every advisory on this library
muteLibrary.title: Mute library
muteLibrary.description: Silences every advisory currently affecting <mono>{pkg}</mono> in this project. Other libraries keep notifying.
muteLibrary.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. waiting for next minor of this library

muteEverywhere.allMutedTrigger: All advisories on this library are already muted across all projects
muteEverywhere.muteAllTrigger: Mute every advisory on this library across all projects
muteEverywhere.title: Mute library across all projects
muteEverywhere.description: Silences every advisory currently affecting <mono>{pkg}</mono> across every project that uses it. Other libraries keep notifying.
muteEverywhere.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. waiting on upstream fix in this library

About.title: About Sentinello
About.intro: Centralized dependency-vulnerability monitoring across your portfolio — self-hosted, on your own infrastructure.
About.notDoBody: Sentinello runs entirely on your infrastructure. It does not read your source code — it discovers projects only by locating package.json files — nothing is uploaded anywhere, no telemetry leaves your machine, there is no central account, and no LLM or AI is involved. It is plain npm audit.
About.whoBody: Built by <company>Walk of Code LLC</company> and Sebastian Arena (<linkedin>LinkedIn</linkedin>).
About.advisoryBody: Vulnerability data originates from the GitHub Advisory Database, reached through the npm registry's advisory endpoint via npm audit. Today this covers the npm ecosystem; additional sources may be added later.

npm.purpose: Fetch advisory data via npm audit

github.purpose: Update check for the top-of-page banner

image.disable: Deployment step — not applicable at runtime

About.dataLivesBody: All scan results are stored in a single local SQLite database. Under Docker it lives in the mounted volume at /app/data; running directly (for example under pm2) it defaults to data/sentinello.sqlite in the project directory. Either way you can point it elsewhere with the SENTINELLO_DB_PATH environment variable. It never leaves your machine.
About.reportingBody: Email <email>info@sentinello.org</email> or open a private advisory via <advisories>GitHub Security Advisories</advisories>.
About.contributingBody: Sentinello is open source (MIT). Issues and pull requests are welcome on <github>GitHub</github>.
About.legalNotice: By using Sentinello you accept the <terms>Terms and Conditions</terms>, <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>, and <disclaimer>Disclaimer</disclaimer>. The software is released under the <license>MIT License</license>.

project.allClearBody: The most recent scan reported no advisories for this project.

library.emptyDescription: This library is not present in any current finding row.
